'''Ian Watson''' (born 27 October 1976) is a professional [[rugby league]] coach who is an assistant coach at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]], and a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international who played as a {{rlp|sh}} and {{rlp|hk}} in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s.

He played for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Workington Town]], [[Swinton Lions]], [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Rochdale Hornets]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]]. Watson was a [[Goal (sports)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicker who holds the appearance record for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] with 30 caps.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

Watson has coached the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] and [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. He is considered one of the best British coaches in the game after taking unfancied Salford and Huddersfield to Finals.

==Background==
Watson was born in [[Salford]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.

==Playing career==
Watson started his professional career with the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford Reds]] coming though from local amateur team [[Salford City Roosters|Eccles RLFC]].

In July 1996, he joined [[Workington Town]] on loan,<ref>{{cite web|title=Gay's good news for Castleford|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/gays-good-news-for-castleford-1329561.html|work=The Independent|access-date=16 February 2014|date=20 July 1996}}</ref> where he played in the [[Super League]] for the first time.

He returned to the Salford Reds for the 1997's [[Super League II]], making 27 appearances for the club.<ref>{{cite web|last=Hadfield|first=Dave|title=Rugby League: Clubs refuse to alter fixtures|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-clubs-refuse-to-alter-fixtures-1138634.html|work=The Independent|access-date=16 February 2014|date=14 January 1998}}</ref>

Following the signings of [[Martin Crompton]] and Josh White, despite being a firm club favourite Watson was deemed surplus to requirements, and was sold to the [[Swinton Lions]] for a fee of £15,000.<ref>{{cite news|last=Fitzpatrick|first=Paul|title=Rugby League: Barrow lifts the Lions First Division: Swinton 41, Keighley 16|newspaper=The Guardian|date=9 February 1998|page=10|id={{ProQuest|245210714}}}}</ref> He spent three years with the Swinton Lions before signing a one-year contract with the [[Widnes Vikings]] for the 2001 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Busy Widnes; Rugby League|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1G1-64823104.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140611063632/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1G1-64823104.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=11 June 2014|work=Coventry Evening Telegraph|access-date=16 February 2014|date=30 August 2000|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

He returned to the Super League a year later, re-joining the Salford City Reds on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|title=Watson returns|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1G1-80767160.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140611063633/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1G1-80767160.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=11 June 2014|work=South Wales Echo|access-date=16 February 2014|date=12 December 2001|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

Watson played out the remainder of his career in the [[British rugby league system#History|Championships]].

==Coaching career==
===Swinton Lions===
In January 2014, Watson was appointed as [[player-coach]] for the Swinton Lions.<ref>{{cite web|title=Swinton chairman resigns as club awaits investment|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_14044-swinton-chairman-resigns-as-club-awaits-investment.html|publisher=Love Rugby League|access-date=16 February 2014|date=18 January 2014}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
He left Swinton Lions in July 2014 to join the Salford Red Devils as an assistant coach.<ref>[http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_15469-ian-watson-signed-by-salford.html Ian Watson signed by Salford]</ref> In late 2015, Watson was promoted to interim head coach after [[Iestyn Harris]] left the club.<ref>[http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ian-watsons-deep-end-salford-9792628 Ian Watson's in at the deep end at the Salford Red Devils but learning from legends]</ref> He was given the job on a permanent basis for 2016, working alongside Director of Rugby [[Tim Sheens]].

He coached the [[Salford Red Devils]] to the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|work=The Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019 |last1=Unwin |first1=Will }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

On 17 October 2020, he coached Salford in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat against [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
[[File:IanWatson1.jpg|thumb|Ian Watson at the Huddersfield Giants]]

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 19 November 2020, Watson was confirmed as the new Huddersfield Giants coach on a three-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Huddersfield hire Ian Watson as new head coach|url=https://www.southwalesguardian.co.uk/sport/national/18884983.huddersfield-hire-ian-watson-new-head-coach/|access-date=25 November 2020|website=South Wales Guardian|date=19 November 2020 |language=en}}</ref>

In round 5 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Watson earned his first win as Huddersfield coach when they defeated [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] 14–13.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12293586/ian-watson-huddersfield-giants-head-coach-feeling-good-after-first-super-league-win-of-2021/|title=Huddersfield Giants head coach feeling good after first Super League win of 2021|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
In Watson's first year in charge, Huddersfield finished the [[2021 Super League season]] in a disappointing 9th place on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-42-leigh-centurions-24-eight-try-giants-finish-with-a-flourish-3388627/|title=Huddersfield Giants 42 Leigh Centurions 24: Eight-try Giants finish with a flourish|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk|date=19 September 2021 }}</ref>

In May 2022, he repeated his earlier success with Salford when he steered [[Huddersfield Giants]] to the [[2022 Challenge Cup Final|cup final]], after defeating [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at [[Elland Road]].  On 28 May 2022, Huddersfield were defeated 16–14 in the [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] by [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }}</ref>
Huddersfield finished the regular season in 3rd and entered the playoffs.

On 11 July 2024, it was reported{{by who?|date=November 2024}} that he had left [[Huddersfield Giants]] by mutual consent. Huddersfield started the season winning 7 from 10 and making the semi-final of the Challenge Cup against Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 July 2024|title=Ian Watson 'stood down' as Huddersfield Giants confirm coaching structure change|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ian-watson-stood-down-as-huddersfield-giants-confirm-coaching-structure-change}}</ref>

===Seattle Seawolves===
On 7 November 2024, it was reported{{by who?|date=November 2024}} that he had taken up the role of assistant coach at American [[Major League Rugby]] side [[Seattle Seawolves]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=7 November 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Huddersfield Giants boss Ian Watson lands shock new coaching role|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-huddersfield-giants-boss-ian-watson-lands-shock-new-coaching-role/}}</ref>

===Hull KR===
On 31 October 2025, it was reported that he had taken up the role of assistant coach at [[Hull KR]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=31 Oct 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Ian Watson lands surprise Super League coaching return for 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ian-watson-lands-surprise-super-league-coaching-return-for-2026}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Watson held the record for having won the most caps for [[Wales rugby league team|Wales]], before being surpassed by [[Rhys Williams (rugby league)|Rhys Williams]] in 2022.

Having made his début against the [[United States national rugby league team|USA]] in [[Philadelphia]] in 1995, he made 30 appearances for Wales, scoring 7 tries and kicking one goal.<ref>[https://www.google.com/hostednews/ukpress/article/ALeqM5h2YRMnDBVogYa3aNuorbg41c1J_w?docId=N0058261286111191710A "Thomas début on Wednesday"]{{dead link|date=June 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} [[The Press Association]] (3 October 2010)</ref>

He played at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite book|first1= Graham|last1= Williams|first2= Peter|last2= Lush|first3= David|last3= Farrar|title= The British Rugby League Records Book|publisher= London League|isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6|year= 2009|pages= 108–114}}</ref><ref name="Statistics at walesrugbyleague.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/cymru/senior-player-database.html|title=Statistics at walesrugbyleague.com|publisher=walesrugbyleague.com|date=18 June 2010|access-date=18 June 2010|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100608093028/http://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/cymru/senior-player-database.html|archive-date=8 June 2010}}</ref>

His final appearance for Wales came during the [[2011 Four Nations]] tournament against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]], and he announced his international retirement shortly after.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/15693300|work=BBC News|title=Briers to retire from Wales duty}}</ref>
